citations.note: Section 2(a)(1)(A): license grant 'limited to NonCommercial purposes only' when sharing Licensed Material.
Section 2(a)(1)(B): adapted material may be produced and reproduced 'for NonCommercial purposes only' (and may not be shared at all).
Section 1(h): 'NonCommercial means not primarily intended for or directed towards commercial advantage or monetary compensation.'
Section 1(h) continued: NonCommercial definition covers exchange of material where no monetary compensation is involved.
Section 2(a)(1)(B): may 'produce and reproduce, but not Share, Adapted Material for NonCommercial purposes only.'
Section 3(a)(1) final clause: 'For the avoidance of doubt, You do not have permission under this Public License to Share Adapted Material.'

permissions.commentary: Private modifications are permitted (you may produce and reproduce Adapted Material non-commercially), but sharing, distributing, or publishing any modified version is forbidden. Treat this as grey because the permission to modify privately coexists with a categorical ban on distributing the result.

citations.note: Section 2(a)(1)(A): right to 'reproduce and Share the Licensed Material, in whole or in part, for NonCommercial purposes only.'
Section 1(i): 'Share' includes reproduction, public display, public performance, distribution, dissemination, communication, or importation.

permissions.commentary: Distribution is permitted only for unmodified Licensed Material and only for NonCommercial purposes. Distributing Adapted Material (derivatives) is forbidden (§3(a)(1) final clause, §2(a)(1)(B)).

citations.note: Section 2(a)(1): worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive, irrevocable license to exercise the Licensed Rights in the Licensed Material.
Section 2(b)(2): 'Patent and trademark rights are not licensed under this Public License.'

permissions.commentary: No patent license is granted. The license explicitly excludes patent rights (§2(b)(2)); use of the material does not carry an express patent grant from the licensor.

citations.note: Section 3(a)(1): 'If You Share the Licensed Material, You must:' (retention obligations follow).
Section 3(a)(1)(A)(ii): must retain 'a copyright notice'.
Section 3(a)(1)(A)(iii): must retain 'a notice that refers to this Public License'.
Section 3(a)(1)(A)(iv): must retain 'a notice that refers to the disclaimer of warranties'.
Section 3(a)(1)(C): must indicate the material is licensed under this Public License and include the text, URI, or hyperlink to it.
Section 3(a)(1)(B) nominally requires an indication of modifications, but §3(a)(1) final clause forbids sharing Adapted Material, so this clause is effectively moot under CC BY-NC-ND.
Section 3(a)(1) final clause: 'For the avoidance of doubt, You do not have permission under this Public License to Share Adapted Material.'

conditions.commentary: Because distribution of modifications is forbidden, the document-changes obligation (§3(a)(1)(B)) has no practical application. Recorded as silent: the license does not create an actionable 'document changes' obligation for permitted distributions (which are by definition unmodified).
CC BY-NC-ND has no ShareAlike element, and because Adapted Material may not be shared at all, the question of an Adapter's License does not arise.

citations.note: Section 3(a)(1): 'If You Share the Licensed Material, You must:'
Section 3(a)(1)(A)(i): must retain identification of the creator(s) and any others designated to receive attribution.
Section 3(a)(1)(A)(v): must retain a URI or hyperlink to the Licensed Material to the extent reasonably practicable.

Section 5(b): 'in no event will the Licensor be liable to You on any legal theory ... for any direct, special, indirect, incidental, consequential, punitive, exemplary, or other losses, costs, expenses, or damages'.
Section 5(b) continued: where a limitation is not allowed in full, it may not apply to You.
Section 5(a): 'the Licensor offers the Licensed Material as-is and as-available, and makes no representations or warranties of any kind'.
Section 5(a): disclaims warranties 'of title, merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, absence of latent or other defects, accuracy, or the presence or absence of errors'.
Section 2(b)(2): 'Patent and trademark rights are not licensed under this Public License.'
Section 2(a)(6): no endorsement; nothing constitutes permission to assert connection with or sponsorship by the Licensor.
